verdict_reasoning: EREG (epiregulin) is an EGF-family ligand encoded as a single-pass type I transmembrane precursor (pro-epiregulin) that is anchored at the plasma membrane before regulated juxtamembrane shedding by ADAM10/17 releases the soluble EGF-like ectodomain. The TM precursor stage supports juxtacrine signaling from the cell surface, but the dominant biological actor is the soluble shed form. This fits the 'transient TM precursor of a shed-ligand-dominant gene' pattern — the TM-anchored form is real and surface-accessible but short-lived relative to the secreted pool, qualifying as contextual/dual_localization.
